@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTCBDXJKQA68WPEE4MJW77ZH
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9980'
+title: Add schema-driven shell op commands to shelltool-cli
+---
+## What
+Surface the shell tool's operations as CLI commands in shelltool-cli, generated from the shell tool's FULL schema via the shared generator (card B). NOTE: unlike code-context-cli, shelltool-cli has NO operation commands today — `apps/shelltool-cli/src/cli.rs` only declares lifecycle commands (Serve/Init/Deinit/Doctor/Completion at :51-98) and there is no `commands/ops` module (`commands/mod.rs` only has doctor/registry/serve). So this card is ADDITIVE: it adds a `shell` op command tree (run/grep history/get lines/list processes/kill process — the `SHELL_OPERATIONS` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/shell/mod.rs`).
+
+Mirror kanban-cli's runtime split (because `cli.rs` is compiled standalone by `build.rs` via `#[path = "src/cli.rs"]` and may only depend on `clap` + `std`):
+- Keep the static lifecycle `cli.rs` for build.rs doc/manpage/completion generation.
+- In `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs`, build the op subcommands at runtime from the shell tool full schema (`ShellTool::operations()` → `_full` generator → `swissarmyhammer_operations::cli_gen::build_commands_from_schema`), add them to the clap `Command`, and on a non-lifecycle match route through `extract_noun_verb_arguments` → `ShellTool::execute`.
+- Add a `commands/ops.rs` (new) for the run_operation glue, registered in `commands/mod.rs`.
+
+Changes:
+- `apps/shelltool-cli/Cargo.toml`: add `swissarmyhammer-operations` dep (currently absent; depends on `swissarmyhammer-tools`/`swissarmyhammer-shell` only).
+- `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs`: switch from `Cli::parse()` (:42) to a built clap command that includes the schema-driven op subcommands, while preserving the lifecycle arms in `dispatch_command` (:123).
+- `apps/shelltool-cli/src/commands/mod.rs` + new `ops.rs`.
+
+If surfacing shell ops as a CLI is deemed out of scope (the brief flags H as adjustable), this card can be deferred — but the shared-generator infra (B) and the other migrations do not depend on it. Confirm with the user whether shelltool should gain a CLI op surface before implementing.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `shelltool` exposes its shell operations as schema-generated subcommands (e.g. `shelltool execute command --command "..."`, matching `SHELL_OPERATIONS` op strings).
+- [ ] Lifecycle commands (serve/init/deinit/doctor/completion) still work; build.rs generation still compiles against the static `cli.rs`.
+- [ ] A generated op invocation reaches `ShellTool::execute` and returns output.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Add `apps/shelltool-cli/src/commands/ops.rs` integration tests (mirroring code-context-cli's `ops.rs` tests): drive a shell op (e.g. `execute command`) through the schema-built tree + `extract_noun_verb_arguments` → `execute`, asserting output and exit code.
+- [ ] Command-tree coverage test: generated nouns/verbs match `ShellTool::operations()` op strings.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p shelltool-cli` passes.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the op-command coverage + execute round-trip tests first, then add the runtime schema-driven tree and ops glue.
\ No newline at end of file

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -34,4 +34,20 @@
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p shelltool-cli` passes.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the op-command coverage + execute round-trip tests first, then add the runtime schema-driven tree and ops glue.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write the op-command coverage + execute round-trip tests first, then add the runtime schema-driven tree and ops glue.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-07 15:58)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/commands/ops.rs:140` — The `parse_args` test helper (build command tree from schema → get matches → `extract_noun_verb_arguments`) is the third copy of the same helper, after the canonical one in the `cli_gen` crate's own tests and shelltool-cli's. Since `cli_gen` is the shared home of `build_commands_from_schema`/`extract_noun_verb_arguments`, the round-trip helper belongs there too rather than being re-pasted per consuming crate. Expose a `pub` test-support helper from `swissarmyhammer_operations::cli_gen` (e.g. `cli_gen::test_support::parse_argv(schema, argv) -> Map<String, Value>`) and have the three test modules call it instead of each re-declaring the same build-then-extract sequence.
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:62` — The entire lifecycle-CLI scaffolding added here (`build_cli` plus `global_flag`, `serve_subcommand`, `init_subcommand`, `deinit_subcommand`, `doctor_subcommand`, `completion_subcommand`, `install_target_arg`, `target_value`, `target_to_scope`, `run_completion`) is a near-verbatim copy of the same functions in shelltool-cli and kanban-cli. The schema-driven operation tree was already factored into the shared `swissarmyhammer_operations::cli_gen` crate; the lifecycle commands that wrap it are now triplicated instead. With three binaries carrying byte-identical copies the rule of three is met, and this is the change that adds the third — the divergence cost (a fix to init/deinit/completion behavior must be applied in three places) lands now. Promote the lifecycle scaffolding into a shared CLI crate next to `cli_gen` (e.g. a `cli_lifecycle` module exposing `build_cli(tool_name, about, schema, &[lifecycle])`, `install_target_arg`, `target_to_scope`, `run_completion`, `global_flag`), parameterized by the tool name/about strings and tool type — the only things that actually differ across the three binaries. Have code-context-cli, shelltool-cli, and kanban-cli all call it.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/commands/ops.rs:116` — The test's doc comment promises to check 'Every SHELL_OPERATIONS op string', but the loop iterates a hardcoded 5-element literal list instead of the canonical SHELL_OPERATIONS table. The two must be kept in lockstep by hand: add a sixth operation to SHELL_OPERATIONS and this coverage test keeps passing while silently no longer covering it — defeating its stated purpose. Derive the expected set from the source of truth: `for op in SHELL_OPERATIONS.iter().map(|o| o.op_string())`. Then the test mechanically covers any future op without edits.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:95` — The lifecycle subcommand builders (serve_subcommand, init_subcommand, deinit_subcommand, doctor_subcommand, completion_subcommand) are near-matches of the kanban/code-context copies, differing only in the embedded binary name and about-text. This is a near-match that should be parameterized (by name + about strings), not a fresh third copy — the completion long_about block in particular is a multi-line template duplicated three times. Add a shared builder set parameterized by binary name (e.g. lifecycle_subcommands(name, about)) in the shared cli_gen/lifecycle module, so the three CLIs assemble their lifecycle surface from one source instead of three copies.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:162` — install_target_arg is a byte-for-byte third copy of the same helper in kanban-cli and code-context-cli. Triplicated lifecycle plumbing means the `[project|local|user]` value set and default must be kept in sync by hand across three crates. Extract install_target_arg into a shared lifecycle-CLI helper (alongside swissarmyhammer_operations::cli_gen, or a small shared module) and call it from all three CLIs instead of copying.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:300` — run_completion duplicates the kanban-cli and code-context-cli versions verbatim (only the schema type alias differs). The 'rebuild full command tree, print completion' logic is now maintained in three places. Provide one shared `run_completion(build_cli_result, shell)` (it already delegates to swissarmyhammer_cli_completions::print_completion_for) and call it from each CLI, rather than copying the function.
+- [ ] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/dynamic_cli.rs:1111` — `build_operation_tool_subcommand` re-implements the name+about+long_about Command preamble that `build_command_base` already encapsulates, adding a 4th copy of `Command::new(intern_string(..)) → .about(..) → .long_about(..)` (also in build_command_base, build_command_from_data, build_tool_subcommand_from_data). Each copy is one more place the interning/Option handling can drift. Construct a `CommandData { name, about: tool.cli_about().map(str::to_string), long_about: Some(tool.description().into()), args: vec![], subcommands: vec![] }`, call `build_command_base(&data)`, then attach the `build_commands_from_schema` noun subcommands — reusing the existing preamble builder instead of a parallel copy.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/board/get.rs:42` — GetBoard::execute is ~110 lines of actual code (lines 42-169, excluding blanks/comments), well over the ~50-line limit. It interleaves several distinct responsibilities — board read, column/project sorting, a basic-shape early return, task enrichment, a count-accumulation loop, column count mapping, and summary arithmetic — which makes it hard to read and test each concern in isolation. Extract the count-accumulation loop (95-106) and column-enhancement map (109-126) into a helper like `count_tasks_by_column(&all_tasks) -> (column_counts, ready_counts, total_ready)`, and pull the basic-shape early return (62-77) into a `basic_board_json(...)` helper. That drops execute below 50 lines and lets each phase be unit-tested directly.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-operations-macros/src/lib.rs:213` — has_serde_default only consumes the `= value` form of unrelated serde items, not the nested-meta form like `rename(serialize = "a", deserialize = "b")`. When such an item is encountered, the callback returns Ok without consuming the `(...)` group, parse_nested_meta then aborts the whole walk, and because the result is discarded with `let _ =` (line 207) the abort is silent. Any `default` appearing AFTER the nested item is never seen, so the field is wrongly marked `required` — the exact mis-classification the change set out to fix. Handle the nested-paren case too, e.g. `else if meta.input.peek(syn::token::Paren) { let _; syn::parenthesized!(_ in meta.input); /* drain */ }`, or more robustly recurse via `meta.parse_nested_meta(...)` for grouped items so the walk never aborts before reaching a trailing `default`.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/schema.rs:475` — New test `serde_defaulted_fields_excluded_from_required_signatures` omits the `test_` prefix that every other test in the same module uses, breaking the module's established naming pattern and making the test set inconsistent to scan/grep. Rename to `test_serde_defaulted_fields_excluded_from_required_signatures` to match the surrounding tests.
\ No newline at end of file

Change the builders to `about: impl Into<String>` (and pass the `String` straight to `.about()`), then delete `intern`/`Box::leak` and pass the `format!(...)` results directly. The leak is bounded (once per process), so this is a cleanliness/correctness nit rather than a blocker.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-operations-macros/src/lib.rs:0` — The six `has_serde_default` tests (bare_default_is_detected, valued_default_is_detected, no_default_is_not_detected, list_valued_item_before_default_does_not_mask_it, bound_item_before_default_does_not_mask_it, non_serde_attribute_ignored) are parallel arms over a known set that differ only in (input attribute, expected bool). That is a case table, not six code paths — adding a future case means copying the boilerplate again rather than appending a row. Consider a table-driven form keeping the scenario docs as a column, e.g. `for (attr, expected, why) in [(parse_quote!(#[serde(default)]), true, "bare default"), ...] { assert_eq!(has_serde_default(&[attr]), expected, "{why}"); }`. Accept `impl Into<clap::builder::Str>` (or document the `'static` constraint as deliberate). Minor: in practice all call sites pass string literals, so this is low-impact.
